title: ConsentCategory
description: Config-backed catalogue of consent categories. Platform-default rows carry tenant_id=NULL and are visible to every tenant. Tenant overrides carry a tenant_id and shadow the platform default (matched on `key`). Categories are seeded by `compliance:seed-consent-categories`; tenants override by copying a platform default and mutating fields.

tenant_id.description: NULL = platform default. Non-null = tenant override.

key.description: Stable snake_case identifier used by ConsentGate + #[ConsentRequired(category:)]. Examples: essential, functional, marketing, analytics, ai_training, minor_parental.

display_name.description: Human-readable label shown in consent UI. Translatable via localization module.

description.description: Long-form explanation displayed on the consent surface. Translatable.

is_default_granted.description: When true, new subjects auto-receive a granted ConsentRecord. `essential` is default_granted=true; every other category defaults to false.

requires_guardian.description: COPPA / FERPA gate. When true, ConsentRecord.creating requires a non-null guardian_user_id + evidence.metadata.

is_system.description: Seeded platform defaults are system=true — cannot be hard-deleted; can be superseded by a tenant override.

allowed_channels.description: Array of notification channels this category gates: mail, sms, push, in_app. Marketing gates all four; analytics gates none (analytics is passive).

indexes.name: consent_categories_tenant_key_uidx
indexes.description: Unique per (tenant, key). Platform default has tenant_id=NULL; tenant override has tenant_id=<uuid>. Postgres treats NULL as distinct, so both can coexist.

indexes.name: consent_categories_system_idx
indexes.description: Fast scan for the seeded defaults during tenant override resolution.

is_platform_default.description: Computed. True iff tenant_id is null.

notes: Every tenant-facing read merges platform defaults with the tenant's overrides at the query layer. The wire shape stays flat — consumers see ONE row per key.
